questions.prompt:
An IAM user is in two groups. One policy allows s3:GetObject on arn:aws:s3:::prod-bucket/*.
Another policy explicitly denies s3:GetObject on arn:aws:s3:::prod-bucket/private/*.
The user tries to download prod-bucket/private/file1.txt. What happens?

options.text: Allowed because group policies merge additively
Denied because explicit Deny overrides Allow
Denied only if a permissions boundary exists

questions.hint: Remember the policy evaluation rule ordering: explicit Deny wins.
questions.explanation: AWS policy evaluation: any explicit Deny overrides any Allow, regardless of where it appears (group, inline, managed).

questions.prompt: An application on EC2 needs to call AWS APIs. The company wants to follow best practices and reduce credential leakage risk. Which options help achieve this? (Select TWO)

options.text: Store access keys in EC2 user data and rotate weekly
Attach an IAM role to the EC2 instance
Use AWS Secrets Manager to store the access keys
Use temporary credentials from the instance metadata service
Use the root account access keys and restrict by IP

questions.hint: Prefer instance roles + temporary credentials over static keys.
questions.explanation: An EC2 instance profile provides temporary credentials via IMDS; using static access keys increases leakage risk.
